description: Grande Oaks Golf Club is a private 18-hole course in Davie with deep roots — originally opened in 1959 as Rolling Hills Country Club, where scenes from the film Caddyshack were shot. Wayne Huizenga purchased the property in 1999, and a complete redesign and rebranding produced the current Grande Oaks layout in 2016. The course is managed by Troon and is reserved exclusively for members and their guests.
notes: City in DB listed as 'Circle Davie' — Circle is the street designation (Rolling Hills Circle), Davie is the city

description: Wynmoor Golf Course is an 18-hole executive course tucked inside the Wynmoor Village, a gated 55+ condominium community in Coconut Creek. Built in 1974, the course is available to community residents and seasonal members, and closes to outside play during peak winter months. It offers a walkable, low-pressure layout suited to the active-adult lifestyle of the surrounding neighborhood.
notes: Residents-only/community course in 55+ gated community; semi-private access seasonally

description: Pine Island Ridge Country Club is a private 18-hole course in Davie, built in 1977 and set within a residential golf community. The club offers a full complement of amenities including dining, swimming, pickleball, and a well-maintained championship layout. Membership is required for access, and the course is not available to the public.

Davie Golf and Country Club is the only public golf course in the Town of Davie, operating as a municipal facility since the Town purchased the former Arrowhead Golf Club in 2011 and completed a full renovation before reopening in November of that year. The 18-hole layout at 8201 Nova Drive offers an accessible daily-fee experience for residents and visitors alike. Originally built in 1973, the course combines a classic Florida layout with modern improvements made during the town-funded overhaul.

notes: permanently closed
permanently closed April 2013

description: Lago Mar Country Club is a 162-acre private club in Plantation, founded in 1969 by Walter Banks and originally tied to the Lago Mar Resort Hotel on Fort Lauderdale Beach. The 18-hole, par-72 championship course was redesigned in 2009 by Kipp Schulties and reopened January 1, 2010, earning design recognition for its reworked bunkering and water features. Membership is required; the club also offers tennis, dining, and aquatics.
notes: Physical address is 500 NW 127th Ave, Plantation FL 33325 — listed as Fort Lauderdale in DB but actually in Plantation

description: Fort Lauderdale Country Club is one of South Florida's oldest private clubs, founded in 1926 and operating continuously from its home at 415 E Country Club Circle. The club features two championship courses and is entirely member-owned, with a reputation for meticulous conditioning and traditional club culture. Non-members have no access to the golf facilities.
notes: Physical address is in the 33317 zip; club spans Fort Lauderdale/Plantation border area

description: Lauderhill Golf Course is a compact 9-hole, par-30 course owned and operated by the City of Lauderhill, located at 4141 NW 16th Street in Lauderhill. Built in 1975, the course plays just 1,200 yards and is designed for beginners, seniors, and golfers looking for a quick round. Greens fees are minimal and no tee time is required for most play.
notes: Course is a City of Lauderhill municipal facility in Lauderhill, FL 33313 — mailing address sometimes lists Fort Lauderdale but the city is Lauderhill

description: Coral Ridge Country Club opened in February 1955, built on land developed by Robert Trent Jones Sr. and his wife Ione, who envisioned a traditional private club anchored by a championship layout of their own design. The club remained in the Jones family until 2004 and later added a 9-hole par-3 course designed by Rees Jones. Located at 3801 Bayview Drive in Fort Lauderdale, the club is private and membership is by invitation.
notes: Founded 1954, opened February 1955; designed by Robert Trent Jones Sr.

description: The Diplomat Golf Resort & Spa traces its origins to the 1958 opening of the Diplomat Hotel and Country Club in Hallandale Beach, where Red Lawrence designed the original 18-hole layout. The course was fully redesigned by Joe Lee and reopened in 2000 as a resort-accessible daily-fee facility. At 501 Diplomat Parkway, the course is open to resort guests and the public.
notes: Physical location is Hallandale Beach, FL 33009 — listed as Hollywood in DB but address is Hallandale Beach; resort hotel is in Hollywood but the golf course address is Hallandale Beach

description: Eco Golf Club is a 9-hole, par-32 executive course at 1451 Taft Street in Hollywood, operated by the City of Hollywood as a public facility. Built in 1982, the course plays 2,265 yards and is widely regarded as one of the more attractive short-course options in South Broward. Walking is permitted, and the accessible format draws beginners, seniors, and players looking to sharpen their short game.
notes: City of Hollywood municipal executive course; also listed as Eco Grande Golf Course by the city

description: Orangebrook Golf & Country Club is Hollywood's historic 36-hole municipal facility, with the original 18-hole course opening in 1933 — making it one of the oldest continuously operated public golf courses in South Florida. The Women's International 4-Ball, the longest-running women's amateur event held at a single location in the United States, has been hosted here, and in 1960 Jack Nicklaus won the Men's Amateur 4-Ball on these grounds. Located at 400 Entrada Drive, Orangebrook remains a city-owned facility currently undergoing a major public-private redevelopment.

notes: permanently closed 2020 — Inverrary Country Club closed permanently under Broward County emergency order during COVID-19 pandemic; property not reopened
permanently closed August 29, 2021; property sold for housing development

description: Oriole Golf Club is an 18-hole public course at 8000 Margate Boulevard, established in 1971 and situated within the Oriole residential community. The layout offers an accessible and well-maintained daily-fee experience in north Broward County, drawing a mix of locals and league players. The club also operates tennis facilities and serves as a neighborhood hub for the surrounding Oriole community.

Parkland Golf & Country Club is a private 18-hole course in Parkland, Florida, designed by Greg Norman and opened in 2004 as the centerpiece of a master-planned residential community. The par-72 layout emphasizes strategic play with water features and wide fairways characteristic of Norman's design philosophy. Access is limited to residents and golf members; non-resident golf memberships are available separately.

Hollybrook Golf and Tennis Club's championship course is an 18-hole layout within a 55+ gated condominium community in Pembroke Pines, open since approximately 1968. The course is available to the public from April 16 through October 31 each year, and reserved exclusively for property owners and renters during the winter season. The community also operates a separate 18-hole executive par-3 course on the same grounds.
notes: Semi-private — public from ~April 16 to Oct 31, residents-only Nov 1 to April 15

description: The Executive Course at Hollybrook Golf and Tennis Club is an 18-hole par-54 layout built in 2014, playing 1,599 yards within the same 55+ community that hosts the championship course. Like the main course, it follows a seasonal access model — open to the public outside of peak winter months and reserved for residents when the snowbird season is active. The compact design serves as an ideal option for beginners and those looking for a shorter round.
notes: Semi-private — same seasonal access policy as the championship course at Hollybrook

description: Pembroke Lakes Golf Club is a city-owned 18-hole public course at 10500 Taft Street in Pembroke Pines, operated by Professional Course Management on behalf of the City. Originally opened in 1969, the course underwent a $7 million renovation in 2007 and now offers a polished daily-fee experience with a driving range and practice facilities. It is one of the better-value public options in south Broward County.

Jacaranda Golf Club in Plantation features two 18-hole championship courses — the East Course, designed by Mark Mahannah and opened in 1970, and the West Course, added in 1975. Both layouts have earned Four-Star ratings from Golf Digest and have hosted PGA, USGA, and FSGA events. The club operates on a semi-private model, accepting public tee times while also hosting members, tournaments, and corporate events; both courses are currently undergoing a major renovation with completion planned for late 2026.
notes: Name in DB is 'Jacararanda' — correct spelling is 'Jacaranda Golf Club'

description: Plantation Preserve Golf Course & Club is a city-owned public facility that opened in February 2006 at 7050 West Broward Boulevard, positioned within a 55-acre Everglades-inspired wetlands preserve. The 18-hole, par-72 championship layout was designed by vonHagge, Smelek & Baril and stretches 7,148 yards from the tips. The City of Plantation operates the course to provide public country club-level conditions without the membership requirement.

The Oaks Course at Palm-Aire Country Club in Pompano Beach is a George and Tom Fazio-designed layout that opened in 1971, stretching 6,910 yards to a par of 71. One of four courses historically operated at the Palm-Aire complex, the Oaks is known for its tree-lined fairways and classic Fazio bunkering. The course accepts public tee times through Troon-managed booking.
notes: Full name: Oaks Course at Palm-Aire Country Club; listed as 'Oaks Course' in DB — confirmed to be the Palm-Aire Oaks at 3701 Oaks Clubhouse Dr, Pompano Beach FL 33069

description: Seven Bridges at Springtree, now operating as The Bridges at Springtree Golf Club, is a city-managed public course at 8150 Springtree Drive in Sunrise. The original course was built in 1972 and significantly renovated in recent years by the City of Sunrise, which now oversees its operation. The layout is a compact, walkable design that draws neighborhood regulars and offers accessible green fees.
notes: Currently operating as The Bridges at Springtree Golf Club, managed by the City of Sunrise; public, not semi-private as originally flagged
permanently closed; property sold to CC Homes in 2023 for 900-unit residential development (Solterra); site subsequently sold to Lennar land bank January 2026

description: The Glades Course at Colony West Golf Club is a par-64 executive layout in Tamarac, playing 4,175 yards and designed by Bruce Devlin and Robert von Hagge. It sits on the west side of Pine Island Road, separate from the Championship Course, and accepts public tee times. The shorter format makes it a popular option for players who want a quick round without sacrificing a well-conditioned track.

Colony West Golf Club's Championship Course in Tamarac is a demanding 7,258-yard, par-71 layout designed by Bruce Devlin and Robert von Hagge, opened in 1971 and originally host to a PGA Tour event. The course is notable for its narrow fairways, deep rough, and aggressive water and bunker placement. Managed by Troon, it accepts public tee times and is considered one of the tougher daily-fee tracks in Broward County.

Woodmont Country Club's Pines 9 is one section of the 27-hole private layout at Woodmont in Tamarac, designed by Robert Von Hagge and opened in 1974. The club operates a restaurant and facilities open to the public, but the golf course itself functions on a semi-private basis with membership pricing and non-member access available at higher daily rates. The Pines is one of three 9-hole combinations at the 27-hole property.
notes: 9-hole segment of Woodmont Country Club's 27-hole facility; semi-private with public access at non-member rates

description: Bonaventure Golf Club (formerly Bonaventure Country Club) is a public 36-hole facility in Weston, designed by Joe Lee and opened in 1970. The East and West courses wind through stands of mature palms and hardwoods around numerous water hazards — the par-3 third hole on one course features a signature waterfall that earned the layout the nickname 'the Green Monster.' Tiger Woods, Sergio Garcia, and Jesper Parnevik have all competed here in the Dixie Amateur.

Weston Hills Country Club's Players Course is a private 18-hole, par-72 layout designed by Robert Trent Jones Jr. and opened in 1995 at The Club at Weston Hills. The 7,272-yard course complements the earlier Tour Course (1990) on the same grounds, giving members two championship options within one gated club. Access is limited to members and their guests; the facility is now managed by Arcis Golf.
notes: Likely the 'Players Course' at The Club at Weston Hills — two courses exist (Tour Course 1990, Players Course 1995); both are private regulation courses

description: The Tour Course at The Club at Weston Hills is a private 18-hole, par-72 layout designed by Robert Trent Jones Jr. and opened in 1990, making it the first of the two championship courses at the facility. Playing 7,069 yards from the tips, the Tour Course has served as a PGA Tour venue and is maintained to tournament standards. The club is private and access is restricted to members and their guests.
notes: Likely the 'Tour Course' at The Club at Weston Hills (opened 1990); companion to the Players Course (1995); both are private regulation layouts at the same address
